ddls:
CREATE TABLE "complaints" (
  "complaint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each complaint */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the complaint was submitted */,
  "borough" TEXT /* Borough where the complaint was made */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the complaint location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the complaint location */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the complaint (e.g., reported, in progress, resolved) */,
  "response_time" INTEGER /* Time taken to respond to the complaint in hours */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who submitted the complaint */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the complaint type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the complaint */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the complaint (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date when the complaint was resolved */,
  "resolution_details" TEXT /* Details of the resolution */,
  "upvotes" INTEGER /* Number of upvotes from other residents */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("complaint_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_complaints_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES complaint_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_complaints_borough FOREIGN KEY ("borough") REFERENCES boroughs ("borough_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_complaints_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "boroughs" (
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each borough */,
  "borough_name" TEXT /* Name of the borough */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for borough issues or inquiries */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the borough */,
  "area" REAL /* Area of the borough in square kilometers */,
  "number_of_complaints" INTEGER /* Total number of complaints in the borough */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("borough_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user submitting the complaint */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user for SMS notifications */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user for better context */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date when the user registered */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_logs" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response log entry */,
  "complaint_id" INTEGER /* ID of the complaint being responded to */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date when the response was recorded */,
  "response_details" TEXT /* Details of the response made to the complaint */,
  "responded_by" TEXT /* Name of the official who responded to the complaint */,
  "response_type" TEXT /* Type of response (e.g., email, phone call, in-person) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_response_logs_complaint_id FOREIGN KEY ("complaint_id") REFERENCES complaints ("complaint_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "complaint_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each complaint type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the complaint type (e.g., Noise, Sanitation) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the complaint type */,
  "service_department" TEXT /* Department responsible for handling the complaint type */,
  "average_response_time" INTEGER /* Average time taken to respond to this type of complaint (in hours) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notification_settings" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for notification settings entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with these settings */,
  "email_notifications" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user wants email notifications */,
  "sms_notifications" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user wants SMS notifications */,
  "push_notifications" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user wants push notifications */,
  "notification_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of notifications (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notification_settings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
